channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack
item.description: Ernst & Young is notifying customers of a data breach caused by the compromise of a third-party support ticket system used by its IT personnel. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Inside the Search for "Clean" Residential Proxies for Carding
item.description: Residential proxies are no longer the silver bullet they once were for carding. Flare explains why cybercriminals increasingly seek "clean" residential proxies and combine them with browser fingerprin...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Coding Tests Deliver OtterCookie-Aligned Malware Hidden in SVG Flag Images
item.description: North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed employing steganography in SVG image files to conceal malicious payloads as part of a campaign using fake job ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Gold Eagle Clearinghouse Targets Security Gap, But How Is Unclear
item.description: The White House launched Gold Eagle to coordinate vulnerability response in a new AI world, but multiple questions linger over how it's being implemented....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Bets 'Agentic Defense' Strategy Can Outpace Attackers
item.description: Google Cloud incorporates key Wiz capabilities into an agentic defense platform to automate threat detection and remediation against AI attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: E.U. Orders Google to Open Android Mic, Camera and Screen to Rival AI Assistants
item.description: The European Commission on Thursday ordered Google to give rival AI assistants the same reach into Android that Gemini already has: the camera, the microphone, whatever is on screen, a wake word that ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Race to Field Military Autonomy Is On, Can Trusted Information Infrastructure Keep Pace?
item.description: Military forces are under increasing pressure to field autonomous capabilities faster than ever before. Across the U.S., UK, and NATO, new investment, evolving defense strategies, and accelerated acqu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges
item.description: A security researcher using the "Nightmare Eclipse" handle has released a Windows zero-day exploit dubbed LegacyHive that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
item.description:
Armenia has held a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in a detention center since June 28, on a U.S. extradition request for a REvil ransomware suspect named Aleksandr Ermakov.
His wife, Maria Y...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Windows Server 2022 reach end of mainstream support in 90 days
item.description: Microsoft announced that Windows Server 2022 will reach the mainstream end date in October 2026, but will switch to extended support and continue receiving security updates for five more years. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
item.description: ACR Stealer, an infostealer in circulation since 2024, is walking out of enterprise networks with saved browser passwords, live session tokens, PDFs, Microsoft 365 documents, and files from synced One...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New GoSerpent Malware Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Diplomats for Espionage
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented malware called GoSerpent that has been put to use in cyber attacks targeting entities in Southeast Asia since late 2025 with a focus...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US charges two over laundering $43 million from investment fraud
item.description: U.S. prosecutors on Thursday charged a New York man and woman for their roles in a large-scale crime ring that laundered money stolen in cyber investment fraud scams. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA urges immediate action on actively exploited Fortinet flaws
item.description: CISA on Thursday ordered government agencies to prioritize patching two actively exploited vulnerabilities in the Fortinet FortiSandbox threat detection platform. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly patched security flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) cata...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New ClickLock macOS malware traps users into revealing login password
item.description: A new macOS information-stealing malware dubbed ClickLock terminates all visible processes to force users into entering their system login password. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Coca-Cola says Fairlife ransomware attack halts US dairy production
item.description: The Coca-Cola Company disclosed today that a ransomware attack impacting its Fairlife dairy subsidiary has disrupted operations, temporarily suspending production of Fairlife products across the Unite...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Agentic AI: Taming the Unpredictable
item.description: Agentic artificial intelligence is creating enough risks for organizations to demand a security reframe....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 1M+ Emails Use Hidden Text to Dupe AI Security Filters
item.description: Artificial intelligence and LLMs can be surprisingly ineffective against text salting, allowing phishing emails to slide right into your inbox....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Claude Chrome extension flaw lets malicious extensions trigger AI actions
item.description: A flaw in Anthropic's Claude for Chrome browser extension could allow a malicious extension to trigger predefined AI actions by simulating user clicks, potentially allowing it to abuse Claude's access...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New OkoBot framework deploys 20 payloads to steal data, crypto
item.description: A new malicious framework called OkoBot is delivering more than 20 payloads in attacks focused on stealing cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases, credentials, and other sensitive data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
item.description:
Owen Flowers, 18, and Thalha Jubair, 20, were each sentenced to five and a half years at Woolwich Crown Court on Thursday, 16 July 2026, for the 2024 hack of Transport for London.
The attack left 148...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ThreatsDay: Game Cheat Spyware, 24-Hour Ransomware, Chrome Sync Stalking + 12 More Stories
item.description:
A lot of this week’s trouble starts with something that looks close enough.
A familiar repo. A useful installer. A harmless sync setting. Then the handoff goes bad, the box starts talking to someone ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI Agents Broke the Security Playbook. Here's What Replaces It.
item.description: Traditional security workflows were built for environments that changed at human speed. Token Security explains why AI agents require a new approach: building on a live identity foundation while givin...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 23andMe to pay $18 million in new genetics data breach settlement
item.description: Genetic testing company 23andMe has agreed to pay $18 million to settle claims from a coalition of 43 attorneys general that it failed to protect customers' genetic data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
item.description: n8n, the workflow automation platform, handed out the wrong accounts at login. On Enterprise instances configured to trust more than one external token issuer, it matched an incoming JWT to a local us...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New TELEPUZ Malware Spreads via ClickFix to Steal Data and Run Commands
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have called attention to a new modular malware called TELEPUZ that's been spreading via websites infected with ClickFix lures since late April 2026.
"The malware is full-fea...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New ClickLock macOS Stealer Kills Apps Every 210ms Until Victims Type Their Password
item.description: ClickLock Stealer, a new macOS infostealer, answers a victim's refusal by killing their apps on a loop until they hand over the login password. It arrives as a command pasted into Terminal, asks for t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Scattered Spider members behind TfL hack get five years in prison
item.description: Two leading members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime collective were sentenced to five years and six months in prison each for hacking Transport for London (TfL) in 2024. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows 11 24H2 Home and Pro reach end of support in 90 days
item.description: Microsoft announced on Wednesday that systems running Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB 2016 and Home and Pro editions of Windows 11 24H2 will stop receiving updates in three months. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
